title: Timbs v. Indiana, 586 U.S. 146 (2019) — full opinion (Cornell Legal Information Institute)
author: Supreme Court of the United States (opinion of the Court by Ginsburg, J.)
organization: Cornell Law School — Legal Information Institute
bias_or_interest: Primary judicial record; Cornell LII is a neutral redistributor.
relevant_locations: Concurrences (Gorsuch, J.; Thomas, J., in the judgment)
quotation_note: Public-domain U.S. government work; short attributed quotes are safe.
rights_note: Opinion text is public domain. Cite Cornell LII as the access source.
notes: Primary opinion (No. 17-1091), 139 S.Ct. 682. Confirms 9-0, Feb 20 2019, Ginsburg; Excessive Fines Clause incorporated; ~$42,058 Land Rover; $10,000 max fine; vacated & remanded. Capture durable hash before publish.

title: Timbs v. Indiana — Oyez case record
organization: Oyez (IIT Chicago-Kent College of Law / Cornell LII / Justia)
bias_or_interest: Educational legal database; secondary summary of the primary record.
quotation_note: Summarize; do not copy long passages.
notes: Cross-check for vote, date, procedural history, and the on-remand outcome (Timbs kept the vehicle).

title: Indiana Civil Forfeiture — Tyson Timbs case page (Institute for Justice)
organization: Institute for Justice (IJ) — libertarian public-interest law firm; Timbs's counsel
bias_or_interest: ADVOCACY: IJ represented Timbs and campaigns against civil forfeiture. Use only for attributed critique and case-background facts, never as neutral fact on the system.
quotation_note: Attribute explicitly ('the Institute for Justice argues'); do not state critique as undisputed fact.
notes: Petitioner's counsel; advocacy source. Forfeiture-mechanics + 'policing for profit' framing must be attributed; defenders' view noted in script.

title: There Is Bipartisan Agreement on the Uncivility of Civil Forfeiture (ACLU)
bias_or_interest: ADVOCACY (civil-liberties). Used only to support the neutral framing that criticism of civil forfeiture is bipartisan / spans the political spectrum.
quotation_note: Attribute; use for the 'bipartisan concern' framing only.
notes: Supports CLM that criticism is non-partisan / spans left and right (conservative groups + ACLU).
